evals.prompt: Initialize aria knowledge for this project.
evals.expected_output: Agent scans the project (tech stack, structure, existing docs), asks interactive questions one at a time, generates a concise project.md (under 50 lines), creates an empty learnings.md template, and shows content for approval before saving.
expectations: Agent scans the project before asking any questions (checks file extensions, package managers, frameworks, existing docs)
Agent reads CLAUDE.md and/or README.md if they exist
Agent asks questions one at a time, not all at once
Agent generates a project.md that is concise (under 50 lines)
Agent creates a learnings.md with an empty template header
Agent shows the generated content and asks for approval before saving
Agent commits the result to git

evals.prompt: Re-run aria setup to update project knowledge.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects existing .aria/ directory, asks for confirmation before proceeding, regenerates project.md from scratch, PRESERVES learnings.md (does not overwrite or delete it), and shows the diff between old and new project.md.
expectations: Agent asks for confirmation before regenerating
Agent preserves learnings.md — does NOT overwrite, delete, or recreate it
Agent regenerates project.md from a fresh scan
Agent shows a diff or comparison between old and new project.md before saving

evals.expected_output: Agent detects that a detailed CLAUDE.md exists, references it in project.md instead of duplicating its content (e.g. 'See CLAUDE.md for build commands'), and only adds information that CLAUDE.md doesn't already cover.
expectations: Agent reads CLAUDE.md and identifies what it already documents
Agent references CLAUDE.md in project.md instead of duplicating content (e.g. 'See CLAUDE.md for build commands' or 'See CLAUDE.md for conventions')
Agent does NOT duplicate build commands, conventions, or other content already in CLAUDE.md
Agent produces a concise project.md focused on what CLAUDE.md does not cover
